ruleset_version: Regelverk 2 - Operativt verifieringsregelverk för Certificate of Origin

certificate.label: Document A
certificate.quality_assessment: High quality, text-based PDF with clear fields and readable content.
certificate.notes: Original certificate issued by West Sweden Chamber of Commerce, Göteborg. Certificate number SEG-26D-467691.

invoice.label: Document B
invoice.quality_assessment: High quality, text-based PDF with clear layout and readable content.

lc_check.lc_verification_note: No Letter of Credit reference found in the certificate. The certificate number SEG-26D-467691 does not contain an LC reference in a bank-term context.

locations_found: Sender block (bottom): 'Bolon AB Industrivägen 12 523 90 Ulricehamn SWEDEN'

pairing_validation.note: Certificate references Invoice 157794; invoice bears number 157794. Consignor BOLON AB is the invoice issuer. Consignee MTR Squared Tiles & Flooring is identified in invoice address fields.

candidates.normalized_text: art nr 117340 solid tin area rug

candidates.normalized_text: 1 pallet 205 x 35 x 45 cm grossweight 31 kg

candidates.normalized_text: po box 450016, warehouse no. 4, plot 71, 24b street, al quoz ind. area, ae- dubai, united arab emirates

candidates.normalized_text: mtr squared tiles & flooring materials trading llc

candidates.normalized_text: solid tin area rug – trimming 50 mm

candidates.raw_text: The exporter of the products covered by this document (customs authorization No. SE/BOS/000954) declares that, except where otherwise clearly indicated, these products are of EU preferential origin.

consignor.certificate_value: BOLON AB, Industrivägen 12, SE-523 90, ULRICEHAMN, Sweden
consignor.invoice_value: Bolon AB, Industrivägen 12, SE-523 90 Ulricehamn, Sweden

comparison_steps.description: Search for consignor name BOLON AB in all invoice pages.
comparison_steps.result: MATCH – 'Bolon AB' found in invoice header, sender block, and EORI/VAT block. Direct match as invoice issuer.

comparison_steps.description: Compare certificate consignor name with invoice issuer name.
comparison_steps.result: MATCH – 'BOLON AB' (cert) matches 'Bolon AB' (invoice) after case normalization.
MATCH – Certificate: 'Sweden', Invoice sender block: 'SWEDEN'. Identical after normalization.

rules_applied.description: Corporate structure – consignor name found in invoice

consignor.debug_explanation: Certificate consignor is 'BOLON AB' at 'Industrivägen 12, SE-523 90, ULRICEHAMN, Sweden'. Invoice is issued by 'Bolon AB' at 'Industrivägen 12, SE-523 90 Ulricehamn, Sweden'. The company name matches exactly after case normalization. The address matches exactly. The country 'Sweden' is explicitly stated in both documents. Direct match as invoice issuer — no further normalization rules needed.

consignee.certificate_value: MTR Squared Tiles & Flooring, Po Box 450016, Warehouse No. 4, Plot 71, 24B Street, Al Quoz Ind. Area, Dubai, United Arab Emirates
consignee.invoice_value: MTR Squared Tiles & Flooring Materials Trading LLC, PO Box 450016, Warehouse No. 4, Plot 71, 24B Street, Al Quoz Ind. Area, AE- Dubai, United Arab Emirates

comparison_steps.description: Identify primary billing field in invoice. Invoice has 'Invoice Address' field with 'MTR Squared Tiles & Flooring Materials Trading LLC'.
comparison_steps.result: Primary billing field identified: Invoice Address contains 'MTR Squared Tiles & Flooring Materials Trading LLC'.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate says 'MTR Squared Tiles & Flooring'. Invoice Address says 'MTR Squared Tiles & Flooring Materials Trading LLC'. Certificate name is a subset — missing 'Materials Trading LLC'.
comparison_steps.result: Certificate name is shorter. Need to check if this is a layout truncation or if the names differ substantively.

comparison_steps.description: Check if certificate text is an exact prefix of the invoice's full name. 'MTR Squared Tiles & Flooring' is an exact prefix of 'MTR Squared Tiles & Flooring Materials Trading LLC'. The certificate box 2 field appears to have limited space, and the name ends cleanly at 'Flooring'. The words 'Materials Trading LLC' are the remaining part. This appears to be a layout truncation — the certificate field cut off the full legal name.
comparison_steps.result: MATCH via layout truncation. Certificate text is an exact initial prefix of invoice name. Truncation appears due to field limitation. No other entity with similar name in invoice. Country matches (UAE).

comparison_steps.description: Certificate: 'United Arab Emirates'. Invoice Address: 'United Arab Emirates'.

rules_applied.description: Priority order – Invoice Address is primary billing field
Layout truncation in certificate – 'MTR Squared Tiles & Flooring' is exact prefix of 'MTR Squared Tiles & Flooring Materials Trading LLC'
Country check – United Arab Emirates matches

consignee.debug_explanation: Certificate consignee is 'MTR Squared Tiles & Flooring' in Dubai, United Arab Emirates. Invoice's primary billing field (Invoice Address) states 'MTR Squared Tiles & Flooring Materials Trading LLC' at the same PO Box and address in Dubai, UAE. The certificate name 'MTR Squared Tiles & Flooring' is an exact initial prefix of the invoice's full name 'MTR Squared Tiles & Flooring Materials Trading LLC'. The missing portion 'Materials Trading LLC' comprises a trading company descriptor and legal form — consistent with layout truncation in the certificate's consignee field. All 4 conditions for layout truncation (4.2.2.1.1) are met: (1) exact prefix, (2) apparent field limitation, (3) no other similar entity, (4) country matches. Additionally, the Delivery Address field in the invoice also contains 'MTR Squared Tiles & Flooring' without 'Materials Trading LLC', confirming the short form is used elsewhere. Confidence slightly below maximum due to the truncation requiring rule application, but all conditions are clearly met.

evidence.raw_text: MTR Squared Tiles & Flooring Materials Trading LLC

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: MISMATCH because 'Materials Trading LLC' is missing from certificate name
rejected_hypotheses.reason_rejected: Rule 4.2.2.1.1 allows layout truncation when certificate text is an exact initial prefix of the full name in the invoice. All conditions are met. Additionally, the Delivery Address block in the same invoice uses the exact same short form 'MTR Squared Tiles & Flooring', confirming both forms refer to the same entity.

goods_description.certificate_value: Art nr 102768 Silver Now 200 cm; Art nr 117340 Solid Tin Area rug
goods_description.invoice_value: 102768 Silver Now 200 cm; 117340 Solid Tin Area rug – trimming 50 mm

comparison_steps.description: Certificate article number 102768. Search in invoice.
comparison_steps.result: MATCH – Invoice line 10 contains article number '102768'. Exact match.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate article number 117340. Search in invoice.
comparison_steps.result: MATCH – Invoice line 30 contains article number '117340'. Exact match.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate: 'Silver Now 200 cm'. Invoice: 'Silver Now 200 cm'.
Certificate: 'Solid Tin Area rug'. Invoice: 'Solid Tin Area rug – trimming 50 mm'.
comparison_steps.result: MATCH – Certificate's main descriptor 'Solid Tin Area rug' is identifiable in invoice. The addition '– trimming 50 mm' is a non-identity-bearing addition per 4.3.4.

rules_applied.description: Priority 1 – Article number exact match for both items
Text matching – identity-bearing main descriptor identified in invoice
Non-identity-bearing addition ('trimming 50 mm') does not affect product identity

goods_description.debug_explanation: Two items in certificate: (1) Art nr 102768 Silver Now 200 cm, (2) Art nr 117340 Solid Tin Area rug. Both article numbers verified via Priority 1 (exact match in invoice). For item 1: invoice shows '102768 Silver Now 200 cm' — exact text match. For item 2: invoice shows '117340 Solid Tin Area rug – trimming 50 mm' — the identity-bearing main descriptor 'Solid Tin Area rug' is exactly present; '– trimming 50 mm' is a non-identity-bearing specification addition per 4.3.4, which does not alter product identity.

evidence.raw_text: Solid Tin Area rug – trimming 50 mm

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: MISMATCH because invoice item 2 has additional text '– trimming 50 mm'
rejected_hypotheses.reason_rejected: Per rule 4.3.4, additions that do not change the product's identity do not cause MISMATCH. 'trimming 50 mm' is a processing specification, not an identity-bearing change. The main descriptor 'Solid Tin Area rug' is exactly identifiable in both documents.

quantity.certificate_value: Item 1: 3,5 lm / 7 m²; Item 2: 6 lm / 1 kg

comparison_steps.description: Certificate box 7 contains two lines of quantities: '3,5 lm / 7 m²' for item 1 and '6 lm / 1 kg' for item 2. These are the verification-basis quantities from box 7.
comparison_steps.result: Box 7 quantities identified for both items.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate: 3.5 lm and 7 m². Invoice: 3,5 lm and 7,00 m².
comparison_steps.result: MATCH – 3.5 lm matches 3,5 lm exactly. 7 m² matches 7.00 m² (truncation of decimals: 7.00 truncated to 0 decimals = 7). Both quantities verified.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate: 6 lm and 1 kg. Invoice: 6,0 lm. Need to verify at least one.
comparison_steps.result: MATCH – 6 lm matches 6.0 lm (truncation: 6.0 to 0 decimals = 6). Per 4.4.3.1, at least one quantity verified is sufficient. The 1 kg value cannot be directly identified in the invoice total row, but no value in the invoice explicitly contradicts it.

rules_applied.description: Box 7 quantities are the verification basis
Occurrence check – numeric values identified in invoice
Multiple quantities – at least one verified per item

quantity.debug_explanation: Certificate box 7 lists: Item 1: '3,5 lm / 7 m²', Item 2: '6 lm / 1 kg'. Invoice shows: Item 1 (102768): '3,5 lm' and '7,00 m²', Item 2 (117340): '6,0 lm'. For item 1: 3.5 lm matches exactly; 7 m² matches 7.00 m² via truncation (7.00 → 7). For item 2: 6 lm matches 6.0 lm via truncation. The '1 kg' in certificate box 7 for item 2 does not have a direct counterpart in the invoice line for 117340, but per rule 4.4.3.1, verifying at least one quantity (6 lm) is sufficient and the invoice does not explicitly contradict the 1 kg value. The package info '1 pallet... Grossweight 31 kg' in box 6 is supplementary and not the box 7 verification basis. All verifiable quantities match.

rejected_hypotheses.hypothesis: MISMATCH because 1 kg for item 2 cannot be found in invoice
rejected_hypotheses.reason_rejected: Per rule 4.4.3.1, when multiple quantity indications exist in the certificate, verifying at least one is sufficient. 6 lm is verified. The invoice does not explicitly contradict 1 kg — it simply does not state a weight for this item. Per 4.4.3.1, 'Övriga kvantitetsuppgifter ska INTE i sig medföra MISMATCH om de inte kan verifieras mot fakturan OCH ingen uppgift i fakturan uttryckligen motsäger dessa'.

country_of_origin.invoice_value: Country of Origin SE (both items); EU preferential origin declaration

comparison_steps.description: Search for explicit origin statements in invoice.
comparison_steps.result: Found: 'Country of Origin SE' for article 102768 and 'Country of Origin SE' for article 117340. Also found EU preferential origin declaration.

comparison_steps.description: Certificate: 'Sweden'. Invoice: 'SE'. SE is the ISO alpha-2 code for Sweden.
comparison_steps.result: MATCH – 'Sweden' and 'SE' are equivalent after ISO code normalization.

comparison_steps.description: Both invoice items show 'Country of Origin SE'. Single origin country (Sweden) is uniform across all items.

rules_applied.description: Origin identified via explicit 'Country of Origin' field per article
Uniform origin – all items show SE

country_of_origin.debug_explanation: Certificate box 3 states 'Sweden' as the country of origin. Invoice explicitly states 'Country of Origin SE' for both article lines (102768 and 117340). SE is the ISO alpha-2 code for Sweden — normalization per 4.5.4 allows this equivalence. Additionally, the invoice contains an EU preferential origin declaration consistent with Sweden being an EU member state. Origin is clearly and unambiguously verified.

rule_evaluation_log.result: NOT_APPLICABLE – No LC reference found in certificate.
PASS – Invoice number 157794 matches, consignor BOLON AB matches, consignee MTR Squared Tiles & Flooring identified.
MATCH – BOLON AB found as invoice issuer.
MATCH – Exact match after case normalization.
Invoice Address identified as primary billing field.
MATCH – Certificate 'MTR Squared Tiles & Flooring' is exact prefix of invoice's 'MTR Squared Tiles & Flooring Materials Trading LLC'. All conditions met.
MATCH – United Arab Emirates in both documents.
MATCH – Both article numbers (102768, 117340) found exactly in invoice.
Applied for '– trimming 50 mm' on item 2.
MATCH – All verifiable quantities found in invoice.

rule_evaluation_log.description: Multiple quantity indications – at least one verified
rule_evaluation_log.result: MATCH – Applied for item 2 (6 lm verified, 1 kg not contradicted).
Found – 'Country of Origin SE' for both items.

overall_assessment.human_explanation: Alla kontrollpunkter matchar. Avsändaren Bolon AB och mottagaren MTR Squared Tiles & Flooring kan verifieras mot fakturan. Varubeskrivning, artikelnummer, kvantiteter och ursprungsland Sverige stämmer överens mellan certifikatet och fakturan. Dokumentparet kan godkännas automatiskt.
overall_assessment.consolidation_note: All five checkpoints verified as MATCH. Consignor is direct match. Consignee matches via layout truncation rule (exact prefix). Goods verified via article numbers and text matching. Quantities verified for both items. Country of origin Sweden verified via ISO code SE in invoice's explicit origin fields.

human_readable_report.title: Verifieringsrapport – Certificate of Origin mot Faktura

sections.certificate_value: BOLON AB, Industrivägen 12, SE-523 90, ULRICEHAMN, Sweden
sections.invoice_value: Bolon AB, Industrivägen 12, SE-523 90 Ulricehamn, Sweden
sections.rule_applied: Direct match as invoice issuer after case normalization
sections.motivation: Certifikatets avsändare 'BOLON AB' i Sverige identifieras direkt som fakturautställare 'Bolon AB' i Sverige. Företagsnamn, adress och land överensstämmer exakt efter normalisering av versaler/gemener.

sections.certificate_value: MTR Squared Tiles & Flooring, Po Box 450016, Dubai, United Arab Emirates
sections.invoice_value: MTR Squared Tiles & Flooring Materials Trading LLC, PO Box 450016, AE- Dubai, United Arab Emirates
sections.rule_applied: Layout truncation – certificate name is exact initial prefix of invoice full name
sections.motivation: Certifikatets mottagare 'MTR Squared Tiles & Flooring' är en exakt inledande del (prefix) av fakturans fullständiga namn 'MTR Squared Tiles & Flooring Materials Trading LLC'. Avkortningen beror på fältbegränsning i certifikatet. Samma kortform förekommer även i fakturans leveransadressblock. Land (United Arab Emirates) stämmer överens. Alla villkor för layoutavkortning (4.2.2.1.1) är uppfyllda.

sections.certificate_value: Art nr 102768 Silver Now 200 cm; Art nr 117340 Solid Tin Area rug
sections.invoice_value: 102768 Silver Now 200 cm; 117340 Solid Tin Area rug – trimming 50 mm
sections.rule_applied: Article number exact match + text matching with non-identity-bearing addition
sections.motivation: Båda artikelnumren (102768 och 117340) matchar exakt mellan certifikat och faktura. Varubeskrivningen 'Silver Now 200 cm' är identisk. 'Solid Tin Area rug' identifieras exakt i fakturan; tillägget '– trimming 50 mm' är en icke-identitetsbärande specifikation som inte ändrar produktens identitet.

sections.certificate_value: Item 1: 3,5 lm / 7 m²; Item 2: 6 lm / 1 kg
sections.rule_applied: Occurrence check with decimal truncation normalization and multiple quantity rule
sections.motivation: Artikel 1: 3,5 lm matchar exakt; 7 m² matchar 7,00 m² efter trunkering av decimaler. Artikel 2: 6 lm matchar 6,0 lm efter trunkering. Vikten 1 kg för artikel 2 saknas i fakturan men motsägs inte heller — per regel 4.4.3.1 räcker det att minst en kvantitetsuppgift verifieras per artikel.

Certifikatets ursprungsland 'Sweden' identifieras i fakturan via explicita fält 'Country of Origin SE' för båda artiklarna. SE är ISO alfa-2-koden för Sverige. Ursprungslandet är enhetligt och verifierat.

output_quality_checks.notes: All checkpoints MATCH with confidence >= 0.85. comparison_result IDENTICAL is consistent with all checkpoints being MATCH. No MISMATCH or MANUAL_REVIEW on any checkpoint.
